seoTitle: How to Add Amazon Income to QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to add your Amazon seller income to QuickBooks Online so your marketplace sales, fees, and payouts are recorded accurately.
intro: Amazon seller payouts bundle sales, fees, and refunds into one deposit, which can get messy if entered incorrectly. This guide covers how to bring Amazon income into QuickBooks Online in a way that keeps everything properly categorized.

steps.heading: Gather your Amazon settlement report
steps.body: Download the settlement or payout report from Amazon Seller Central covering the deposit you're recording.

steps.heading: Set up income and fee categories
steps.body: Create or confirm accounts for Amazon sales income and Amazon fees so they can be tracked separately.

steps.heading: Record the deposit with a breakdown
steps.body: Enter the deposit in QuickBooks Online, splitting it into gross sales, fees, and any other line items from the settlement report.

steps.heading: Reconcile against your bank feed
steps.body: Match the recorded deposit to the actual bank transaction to confirm everything ties out.

faq.question: Can I connect Amazon directly to QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: Some third-party apps offer a direct connection between Amazon and QuickBooks Online, though manual entry from settlement reports also works.

faq.question: Why shouldn't I record just the net deposit as income?
faq.answer: Recording only the net amount hides your actual sales and fees, making it harder to see true profitability.

faq.question: How often does Amazon pay out?
faq.answer: Amazon typically pays out on a set schedule, often every two weeks, though this can vary by account.

faq.question: Do I need to track Amazon sales tax separately?
faq.answer: Since Amazon often collects and remits sales tax on your behalf in many states, it's worth tracking those amounts separately from your own income.
